methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy

data.narrative:
Newcleo is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised €537M to date across a founding team of three. The valuation exercise examines a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector contains only 1 same-stage Series B comparable, 1 adjacent-stage peer, and no similarly-tiered private companies meeting narrow filtering criteria. This subset of 2 datapoints falls below the minimum threshold of 3 required for reliable median-based valuation. Attempting to broaden the filter to all 8 nuclear energy privates would introduce excessive heterogeneity across stage and capital structure, yielding results too loose to be analytically sound.

Without a sufficient narrow peer set, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range via traditional multiples-based methods. Forward progress requires either: expansion of the comparison set through broader sector inclusion (energy transition, deep tech infrastructure), application of discounted cash flow modeling if financial projections are available, or assessment against the 3 public nuclear comps despite their different stage and market conditions. Key uncertainties include the wide variance in nuclear technology maturity across the peer set and the limited market comparability of early-stage private nuclear ventures.
